$values.Text: VeloxDev is a set of development tools for modern .NET. Built on Source Generators, it consolidates MVVM, animation, theming, frame loops, AOP, Workflow, and Agent capabilities into a single unified abstraction.
Start with "Quick Start" to learn each module step by step. "APIs" gives you a per-module type and attribute reference. "Agent" shows how to expose any .NET object or workflow to an LLM.

$values: Add [VeloxProperty] to fields and [VeloxCommand] to methods to auto-generate properties and async commands
State-interpolation animation: snapshots, fluent composition, cross-platform dispatch
[ThemeConfig] declares multiple themes with gradient transitions and runtime overrides
Update / FixedUpdate frame loops with global throttling and pause
Runtime proxies: pre / override / post hooks for properties and methods
Generate reflection-preserving code for trimming and NativeAOT scenarios
Tree / Node / Slot / Link + Helper graph-based workflow kernel
Zero-dependency reflection toolkit + MAF Function Calling tools

$values.Text: The sub-pages below are organized by module. Each page follows the order "concept → minimal example → advanced". All snippets come from real demos in the Examples folder and can be copied and run as-is.
VeloxDev's MVVM toolkit replaces hand-written boilerplate with a Source Generator: field + attribute → automatic properties, async commands, and collection notifications. No base class required.
Add [VeloxProperty] to a private field, and the corresponding public property and INotifyPropertyChanged notification are generated at compile time.
The generator also emits OnXxxChanging / OnXxxChanged partial methods for each property; implement them as needed.
Add [VeloxCommand] to a Task method to auto-generate an ICommand with validation, concurrency, and lifecycle support.
If a [VeloxProperty] field type implements INotifyCollectionChanged, the generator automatically subscribes to collection events and dispatches them to fine-grained hooks.
A workflow is a graph composed of Tree, Node, Slot, and Link. Components only hold state and commands; behavior is injected via Helpers.
A workflow is not a procedural script but a graph of components:

$values: Workflow container that holds all Nodes / Links and provides Undo / Redo / Submit
Node with Anchor / Size / Slots that carries business logic
Input/output connection point on a node, tracks Targets / Sources
Edge between a Sender and Receiver

$values.Text: Component = ViewModel (state + commands) + Helper (behavior) + StandardEx (default implementations).
Use WorkflowBuilder.ViewModel.* attributes to specify the component type and its Helper. The generator fills in properties, commands, and bridge code.
Swap only the Helper when inheriting

$values.Code:
[WorkflowBuilder.ViewModel.Node<SpecialNodeHelper>]
public partial class SpecialNode : BaseNode { }

$values.Text: A Helper is the behavior object of a component. Inherit a standard Helper and override as needed to replace business logic.
Forward propagation flows along Slot.Targets, reverse propagation flows along Slot.Sources, and every edge first goes through ValidateBroadcastAsync.
Animation as a "state stream": a Snapshot records the current state and a Transition interpolates between states.
Three granularities of property snapshots, used for rollback or as the starting point of an animation.
Compose multi-segment animations with Property / Effect / AwaitThen.

$values.Code: Transition.Exit(rec, IncludeMutual: true, IncludeNoMutual: false);

$values.Text: Use [ThemeConfig] to declare multiple theme mappings on a class in one line, and combine with ThemeManager for switching and gradients.
[ThemeConfig] binds a property to multiple values across themes; a Converter handles construction.
Unity-like frame loop: mark a class with [MonoBehaviour] and implement Awake / Start / Update / FixedUpdate.
Call InitializeMonoBehaviour() in the constructor to register; partial methods are dispatched per frame automatically.
[AspectOriented] marks a member as proxy-able; pre / override / post hooks fire through the proxy object.
The three slots are (start, coverage, end). The return value of one hook is passed as the previous parameter of the next.

$values.Code:
data.Proxy.SetProxy(
    ProxyMembers.Setter, nameof(TeamViewModel.Name),
    start: null,
    coverage: null,
    end: (args, _) => { Console.WriteLine(args[0]); return null; });

teamProxy.Name = "New Team"; // must use the proxy object

$values.Text: In trimming / NativeAOT scenarios, mark a type with [AOTReflection] and the generator preserves the required reflection metadata at compile time.
A per-module cheat sheet: read "Quick Start" first to learn the usage, then come back here for parameters and signatures.

$values: Suffix of the command property name; Auto derives it from the method name
When true, generates a CanExecuteXxxCommand partial method
Create a slot on a node
Record snapshots of the specified properties
Record all properties except the specified ones
Declare the final state of a target property
Bind ease / duration / loop / FPS to the current segment
Chain the next animation segment (with optional delay)
Inject the platform interpolator (required for animation)
Animation start point: Cache or Reflect
Initialize the theme; must run after InitializeComponent
Callbacks before and after the switch
Time of the previous frame (ms, with TimeScale applied)
Whether the frame has been handled
Intercept property writes (args[0] is the new value)

$values.Code:
void SetProxy(
    T target,
    ProxyMembers memberType,
    string memberName,
    ProxyHandler? start,
    ProxyHandler? coverage,
    ProxyHandler? end);

delegate object? ProxyHandler(
    object?[]? parameters,
    object? previous);

$values: Namespace of the generated class; Auto derives the longest common prefix

$values.Text: The generator emits public static class AOTReflection with an Init() method into {Namespace}.AOTReflection.g.cs; call it once at startup to ensure the metadata is preserved.
VeloxDev.Core.AI provides a zero-dependency reflection toolkit; VeloxDev.Core.Extension wraps any .NET object or an entire Workflow Tree as a Microsoft.Agents.Framework Function Calling toolset.

$values: Property read/write, batch Patch, type conversion, blacklist
Method invocation, overload resolution, default parameters

$values.Text: Wrap any .NET object as 10 MAF AITools (property read/write, command execution, method invocation) in one line, not limited to workflow scenarios.

After binding a Tree, it provides 59+ Function Calling tools that cover query, mutation, connection, execution, undo/redo, state snapshots, batch operations, cloning, graph traversal, auto layout, and more.
